heading: Remove the limits
intro: The online version has a limit so it can stay free for everyone. Run your own copy and the limit is up to you — and it's still free.

easy.title: Put it online in 1 click
easy.intro: Railway creates your own copy and gives you a link to use and share it. There's a free plan to get started.
steps: Create a free Railway account (with your email or your GitHub account).
Wait a couple of minutes. Done: you'll have your own link to use Croma.
easy.note: Later you can raise the limit from the Railway dashboard. No coding needed.

local.badge: For anyone who wants it on their computer
local.title: Install it on your computer
local.intro: A few more steps, but all free and 100% private (your images never leave your machine). Pick your system:

node.title: 1. Install Node.js
node.desc: It's the program Croma needs to run. Download the version marked “LTS” and click Next until it finishes.

download.title: 2. Download Croma
download.desc: Get the project and unzip the folder wherever you like (your Desktop, for example).

open.title: 3. Open a terminal inside that folder

run.title: 4. Start Croma
run.desc: Type the first line and press Enter; when it finishes, type the second. Keep it open while you use Croma.
run.openAt: When it tells you to, open this address in your browser:

os.mac: Open the “Terminal” app, type cd followed by a space, drag the Croma folder onto the window, and press Enter.
os.windows: Open the Croma folder, right-click inside it and choose “Open in Terminal”.
os.linux: Right-click inside the Croma folder and choose “Open terminal here”.
